title: corydalis pterygopetala subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Corydalis pterygopetala subsp. macrocarpa is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family, native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids, including berberine and protopine. The health benefits of Corydalis pterygopetala subsp. macrocarpa include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and relaxation of the muscles, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and muscle spasms. Corydalis pterygopetala subsp. macrocarpa is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in various pharmaceutical formulations.
